channel.title: dotnet/wpf Pull Requests
channel.description: Pull Requests from https://github.com/dotnet/wpf, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: PR: Use preview language version for WPF - @ryalanms
item.description:
Closes #4748

Update language version to support implicit global namespace feature in .NET 6.0 Preview 7.

(Waiting on test pass results...)

item.title: PR: Revert "Remove analyzers from framework references" - @ericstj
item.description:
Reverts dotnet/wpf#4849

This should no longer needed now that WPF is consuming an RC SDK. Don't merge this until we manually validate the C++/CLI binaries don't have references to the JSON source generator (since that would only fail after ingestion upstack).

item.title: PR: Cherry-pick 'Only enable `DisableImplicitNamespaceImports` when `UseWPF` is set #4868' from main - @ryalanms
item.description:
Cherry-pick "Only enable DisableImplicitNamespaceImports when UseWPF is set #4868" from main.

WinForms project also import Microsoft.WinFX.targets, and WinForms now relies on implicit imports. Scope disabling implicit imports to WPF. This broke WinForms VB projects.

But it wasn't @JunTaoLuo's change(s). It's actually caused by DisableImplicitNamespaceImports here: ff1be8e#diff-681ca681001fbc20488df7fdbb46a8c40ac2df9dc2210df5ad869aee6a56df16R23 This disables these VB imports: https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/blob/55195f3c4ba3f582aa91ec824bd24a7ba3e73528/src/Tasks/Microsoft.NET.Build.Tasks/targets/Microsoft.NET.Sdk.DefaultItems.props#L118-L129

/cc @dotnet/wpf-developers

Required to unblock: https://github.com/dotnet/installer/pull/11143.

item.title: PR: Only enable `DisableImplicitNamespaceImports` when `UseWPF` is set - @ryalanms
item.description:
WinForms project also import Microsoft.WinFX.targets, and WinForms now relies on implicit imports. Scope disabling implicit imports to WPF. This broke WinForms VB projects.

But it wasn't @JunTaoLuo's change(s). It's actually caused by DisableImplicitNamespaceImports here: dotnet/wpf@ff1be8e#diff-681ca681001fbc20488df7fdbb46a8c40ac2df9dc2210df5ad869aee6a56df16R23 This disables these VB imports: https://github.com/dotnet/sdk/blob/55195f3c4ba3f582aa91ec824bd24a7ba3e73528/src/Tasks/Microsoft.NET.Build.Tasks/targets/Microsoft.NET.Sdk.DefaultItems.props#L118-L129

/cc @dotnet/wpf-developers

item.title: PR: Update branding for .NET 6.0 to rc1 - @ryalanms
PR: Support for new UIA events - @SamBent
item.description:
Add support for events in uiautomationcore added since RS2:

  1. Notification event (added in RS3)
  2. ActiveTextPositionChanged event (added in RS5)

See #4824 for the spec.

item.title: PR: Remove analyzers from framework references - @ericstj
item.description:
These are incorrectly raised as references in pre-6.0-preview7 SDKs.

Fixes broken build dotnet/windowsdesktop#1803 by removing these references from WPF's C++/CLI assemblies.

#4848 tracks undoing this.

item.title: PR: Disable implicit namespace imports - @ryalanms
item.description: WPF applications will not build with the most recent SDK.

item.title: PR: Merging internal commits for release/3.1 - @vseanreesermsft
PR: Merging internal commits for release/5.0 - @vseanreesermsft
PR: Spec for automation update - @SamBent
item.description: Spec for refresh of automation wrapper APIs.

item.title: PR: Clean up yaml and add internal runtime download - @mmitche
item.description:
• Remove some unused variables and unused yaml
  • Fix up the internal runtime download args so they are always present when running on the internal project

item.title: PR: Update FxCop analyzers - @ThomasGoulet73
item.description:
Description

Update Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.FxCopAnalyzers to 3.3.1 and activate valid rules. The last version before the deprecated warning. I plan on doing a follow-up PR on chaning FxCopAnalyzers for NetAnalyzers.

Customer Impact

None, there are no behavior changes. Except here: https://github.com/dotnet/wpf/pull/4800#discussion_r664223073.

Regression

None.

Testing

Local build.

Risk

None.

item.title: PR: Fix GetEntryAssemblyLocation Uri - @lindexi
item.description:
Fixes Issue #4781

Description

We do not escape the path string in GetEntryAssemblyLocation method. And it will break when the application in the special path, such as the path contains a #. See https://github.com/dotnet/wpf/issues/4781

Customer Impact

All the GetContentStream

Regression

No.

Testing

Just CI

Risk

item.title: PR: Remove the not accessed local variable in BitmapDecoder.SetupDecoderFromUriOrStream - @lindexi
